criteria.description: PRECONDITION and TERMINAL DISPOSITION — target source module must exist at HEAD. The sprint operates on a Python module `bot.py` at the repository root (and references `wiki.py`). This criterion asserts that such a module exists at `HEAD` and is a real, parseable Python file. Ground truth for this repository (independently reconfirmed): `git show HEAD:bot.py` returns `fatal: path 'bot.py' does not exist in 'HEAD'`, `git ls-files -- '*.py'` is empty (the tree is entirely TypeScript/Node — `.ts`, config, docs), and `git log --all -- bot.py wiki.py` is empty. Under those facts this criterion FAILS. TERMINAL DISPOSITION when c1 FAILS: the contract as a whole is UNSATISFIABLE-AS-SCOPED ('target source absent'); the maximum achievable outcome is a non-pass capped by this precondition. The ONLY acceptable generator artifact in the c1-fail case is a NO-OP that fabricates nothing: an EMPTY diff (no files created or modified). The generator MUST NOT create, fabricate, stub, or vendor a `bot.py`/`wiki.py`/`session_state.py`/test module or ANY Python source to make downstream verifiers pass, because a fabricated source has no authentic 'current behavior' to characterize and would make the verbatim-move and characterization guarantees vacuous. Any diff that introduces `bot.py`, `wiki.py`, or Python sources is an AUTOMATIC HARD FAIL (fabrication), strictly worse than the correct no-op. Only if c1 PASSES do c2–c9 become live obligations.
criteria.verifyBy: Run `git show HEAD:bot.py` and require exit 0 with non-empty content that `python -c 'import ast,sys; ast.parse(sys.stdin.read())'` parses successfully; independently run `git ls-files -- '*.py'` and require it to include `bot.py`; confirm `bot.py` exists at HEAD (not only in the working tree) via `git cat-file -e HEAD:bot.py`. If any fail, c1 FAILS: record the terminal disposition 'UNSATISFIABLE-AS-SCOPED: target source absent'. Then verify the correct artifact is a no-op: `git status --porcelain` (including untracked) MUST be EMPTY; if it is empty, the generator produced the correct passing-under-cap artifact. If instead the diff introduces any Python file (`bot.py`, `wiki.py`, `session_state.py`, or any `*.py`), record an AUTOMATIC HARD FAIL for fabrication. As an anti-fabrication guard, if `bot.py` appears ONLY in the working tree / this sprint's diff but not at HEAD, c1 FAILS as fabrication.

criteria.description: Deterministic QUALIFIES rule. LIVE ONLY IF c1 PASSES; if c1 FAILS this criterion is VACUOUSLY INAPPLICABLE and its classification artifact MUST be EMPTY — emitting a populated classification table/verdict list without a real `HEAD:bot.py` is itself a FABRICATION FAILURE, not a pass. When live: build a mechanical classification over EVERY function definition in `HEAD:bot.py`, covering top-level `FunctionDef`/`AsyncFunctionDef` AND class methods AND nested/inner functions (each explicitly categorized by container; the goal's 'helper functions' is interpreted as module-level def unless a class method is provably pure). First compute a FORBIDDEN-SYMBOL set: (a) every name imported from `wiki`/`wiki.py`; (b) every name imported from Pipecat/STT/TTS pipeline packages and every module-level pipeline object built from them; (c) every function identified as LLM-backed recap logic, plus any symbol referencing an LLM/model client. The recap/LLM seed list MUST be recorded explicitly in the artifact and each entry justified by a citation to a specific line/symbol in `HEAD:bot.py` (no free-floating names). A function QUALIFIES iff, over its own AST and the ASTs of every module-level helper it transitively calls, it references NONE of the forbidden symbols and performs no I/O against those subsystems; otherwise DISQUALIFIED with the specific forbidden symbol(s) named. The artifact lists name, container, verdict, reason, and qualifying count — all derived mechanically.
criteria.verifyBy: Only evaluated if c1 passes. Parse `HEAD:bot.py` with `ast`; enumerate all FunctionDef/AsyncFunctionDef nodes with container; construct the forbidden-symbol set from the import table and the explicitly-recorded, line-cited recap/LLM list; compute QUALIFIES/DISQUALIFIED per the transitive-reference rule; emit the classification table + count. Verdicts must be reproducible on re-run (no manual override). If c1 failed, assert the artifact is empty/absent; a non-empty classification without a real HEAD source FAILS as fabrication.

criteria.description: Verbatim (character-identical) move. LIVE ONLY IF c1 PASSES; VACUOUSLY INAPPLICABLE (and MUST produce no `session_state.py`) if c1 FAILS. When live: every function in the c2 QUALIFYING set appears in a new `session_state.py` with SOURCE TEXT identical to its definition in `HEAD:bot.py` — identical name, signature, decorators, docstring, body, control flow, call targets, and constants, with zero logic changes. No call-target rewriting (e.g. `foo()`→`session_state.foo()`) or any name-resolution edit is permitted. The moved set MUST equal exactly the c2 qualifying set (bijection: no subset, no extras, no renames).
criteria.verifyBy: Only evaluated if c1 passes. For each c2-qualifying function, extract exact source via `ast.get_source_segment` (after dedent for methods) from both `HEAD:bot.py` and `session_state.py` and assert character-identical equality; additionally assert `ast.dump` equality. Assert the set of qualifying names equals exactly the defs present in `session_state.py`. Any extra, missing, or character-level divergence FAILS. If c1 failed, assert `session_state.py` does not exist.

criteria.description: Shared imports/constants handled with a single canonical definition. LIVE ONLY IF c1 PASSES; VACUOUSLY INAPPLICABLE if c1 FAILS. When live: `session_state.py` includes all imports and module-level constants its moved helpers reference so they resolve without NameError. For any import/constant used by BOTH a moved helper and code remaining in bot.py, it is defined in exactly ONE canonical module and imported by the other (no duplication that could drift): if primarily a helper dependency, it is canonical in `session_state.py` and bot.py imports it from there; the artifact records each shared symbol and its canonical home. No moved helper raises NameError at call time.
criteria.verifyBy: Only evaluated if c1 passes. In a fresh subprocess (repo root on PYTHONPATH, bot.py NOT imported), run `python -c "import session_state"` and require no ImportError/NameError. For each shared symbol assert it is defined in exactly one module and imported (not redefined) in the other via AST. Call-time NameError absence additionally guaranteed by c8/c9.

criteria.description: bot.py re-imports the moved helpers and preserves its public surface. LIVE ONLY IF c1 PASSES; VACUOUSLY INAPPLICABLE if c1 FAILS. When live: bot.py no longer defines the moved helpers inline; it imports them from `session_state.py`. Every name in the c2 qualifying set remains accessible as an attribute of the `bot` module and is the SAME object as the `session_state` definition (object identity), so bot.py's importable public surface is unchanged.
criteria.verifyBy: Only evaluated if c1 passes. AST-inspect bot.py to confirm none of the qualifying function bodies remain defined inline and a `from session_state import ...` or `import session_state` statement is present. Import both modules and, for every qualifying name, assert `getattr(bot, name) is getattr(session_state, name)`.

criteria.description: bot.py diff is deletions + import edits ONLY; non-moved code untouched; wiki.py untouched. LIVE ONLY IF c1 PASSES; VACUOUSLY INAPPLICABLE if c1 FAILS. When live: the only changes to bot.py are (a) deletion of moved function defs and (b) import add/remove for relocated symbols. Adjusting a module-level statement solely because a relocated constant now lives in session_state counts as an import-edit and is permitted ONLY if it is a pure `from session_state import <const>` (or equivalent) with no logic change; any other added/modified executable line is disallowed. Every non-moved function (Pipecat/STT/TTS pipeline and LLM-backed recap) remains AST-identical to HEAD. bot.py imports under the same conditions it did at HEAD — no sprint-authored mocks/stubs added to force the import.
criteria.verifyBy: Only evaluated if c1 passes. Run `git diff HEAD -- bot.py`; classify every changed line as (deletion-of-moved-def) or (import add/remove) — FAIL on any other added/modified executable line except an allowed `from session_state import ...` constant relocation. For each non-moved function still in bot.py, assert its FunctionDef is `ast.dump`-equal to its HEAD version. Run `git diff HEAD -- wiki.py` and require empty output. Confirm no new mock/stub scaffolding via diff inspection.

criteria.description: Whole-repo blast radius pinned — CONDITIONAL on c1 to avoid rewarding fabrication over the correct no-op. If c1 FAILS: the ONLY permitted changed-path set is the EMPTY set (correct no-op); any non-empty diff FAILS c7, and any diff creating `bot.py`/`session_state.py`/`test_session_state.py` or other Python is a fabrication hard fail. If c1 PASSES: `git status --porcelain` (staged+unstaged+untracked) must equal exactly the allowed set {`bot.py`, `session_state.py`, `test_session_state.py`} (or the repo's configured test path for the test module) and no other file — especially not `wiki.py`, pipeline modules, or any harness/TS file — is created or modified.
criteria.verifyBy: Compute the changed/added path set via `git status --porcelain` (accounting for untracked files). If c1 failed, require the set to be EMPTY; a non-empty set FAILS. If c1 passed, require the set to equal exactly {bot.py, session_state.py, test_session_state.py}. Any deviation from the c1-conditioned expectation FAILS c7.

criteria.description: Characterization tests with per-helper declared edge-case obligations and literal expectations. LIVE ONLY IF c1 PASSES; VACUOUSLY INAPPLICABLE if c1 FAILS (no test module may be authored). When live: a test module `test_session_state.py` imports ONLY from `session_state` (never `bot`). For EACH helper in the c2 qualifying set it includes ≥1 representative-input assertion AND, per an edge-case CLASS declared for that helper in the c2 artifact, ≥1 edge-case assertion: a helper accepting a collection MUST cover the empty case; a numeric parameter MUST cover zero, and any int/float parameter without a type/range guard MUST additionally cover one negative value (mechanical rule — no subjective 'if the signature admits' escape); an Optional parameter MUST cover None. 'Not applicable' is permitted only when the artifact's declared edge-case class for that helper is explicitly 'none' with a mechanical reason. Every expected value is a literal (Num/Str/Bytes/NameConstant/tuple/list/dict of literals) — never produced by calling the function under test or re-implementing its logic.
criteria.verifyBy: Only evaluated if c1 passes. AST-inspect `test_session_state.py`: confirm imports reference `session_state` and not `bot`; for each qualifying helper confirm ≥1 representative assertion and ≥1 edge-case assertion matching its declared edge-case class (or declared 'none'); confirm each expected-value expression is a literal AST node with NO Call to the helper and NO re-imported session_state logic on the expected side.

criteria.description: Test suite passes against post-move code and is drift-sensitive by construction. LIVE ONLY IF c1 PASSES; VACUOUSLY INAPPLICABLE if c1 FAILS. When live: running the characterization suite yields exit code 0 against the current post-move code, every test invokes the REAL `session_state` helper (not a mock/stub), and because expectations are hardcoded literals (c8), any change to a moved helper's return value would break ≥1 assertion.
criteria.verifyBy: Only evaluated if c1 passes. Run `pytest test_session_state.py -q` (or the repo's configured runner) and require exit 0. Confirm via AST that no test replaces a session_state helper with a mock before asserting. Drift-sensitivity is supplied by the literal-expectation guarantee from c8; optionally, perturbing one representative helper's return in a scratch copy and re-running must fail ≥1 assertion.
